content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance                Administration                Executive/Legal                Enforcement
(916) 322-5662                    322-5660                     322-5901                       322-6441


                                    October 12, 1984


Karen Stauffer
Personnel Analyst
City of La Mesa
P.O. Box 937
La Mesa, CA  92041-0314

                                    Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                         Our File No. A-84-258

Dear Ms. Stauffer:

     This is in response to your recent letter and our telephone
conversation regarding the filing responsibilities for the newly
created position of Commissioner Emeritus to the Parks and
Recreation Commission. As I mentioned on the phone, without
knowing specifically what role, if any, the Commissioners
Emeriti will play on the Commission, it is difficult to be
specific.

     You mentioned the possibility that the Commissioners would
not even attend the meetings. If that is the case, then it
would appear they would have no filing responsibility. Should
the Commissioners participate in the meetings, however, the
extent of their participation could trigger a filing
obligation. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(c)(2) states:

     (c) A public official or designated employee
"participates in the making of a governmental decision"
when, acting within the authority of his or her
position, he or she:

                                * * *

          (2) Advises or makes recommendations to the
decision-maker, either directly or without
significant intervening substantive review, by:

               (A) Conducting research or making any
investigation which requires the exercise of
judgment on the part of the official or
designated employee and the purpose of which
is to influence the decision; or

Karen Stauffer
October 12, 1984
Page 2


                (B)  Preparing or presenting any report,
          analysis or opinion, orally or in writing,
          which requires the exercise of judgment on
          the part of the official or designated
          employee and the purpose of which is to
          influence the decision.

     If the level of participation by the Commissioners
Emeriti meet the above criteria, then it appears they would
have a filing obligation.

     If you have any additional questions or would like
further assistance, please let me know.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [illegible]
                                          Susan M. Harrigan
                                          Administrative Assistant

SMH:plh

F P P C

UEP 11 4 28 PH 04

CITY OF LA MESA

September 4, 1984


Mr. John Keplinger
Executive Director
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Mr. Keplinger:

Our Council recently created the title "Commissioner Emeritus"
and so named two retiring Parks and Recreation Commissioners.

Is there any applicable filing requirement for such a designation? The retired Commissioners will no longer be compensated,
nor will they be voting members.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Karen Stauffer
Personnel Analyst

KS/da


ADMINISTRATION BUILDING, 8130 ALLISON AVENUE, P.O. BOX 937, LA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92041-0314 / (619) 463-6611

sections.question: Is there any applicable filing requirement for the newly created position of Commissioner Emeritus to the Parks and Recreation Commission?
sections.conclusion: If the Commissioners Emeriti do not participate in Commission meetings, they would have no filing responsibility. However, if they participate in the meetings, the extent of their participation could trigger a filing obligation under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(c)(2), which requires filing if they advise, make recommendations, conduct research, prepare reports, or present analyses that are intended to influence governmental decisions.
sections.analysis:
or opinion, orally or in writing, 
which requires the exercise of judgment on 
the part of the official or designated 
employee and the purpose of which is to 
influence the decision. 
If the level of participation by the Commissioners 
Emeriti meet the above criteria, then it appears they would 
have a filing obligation.
sections.question_synthetic: Must individuals holding the newly created position of Commissioner Emeritus file disclosures, given that they are uncompensated and non-voting?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Filing responsibility depends on the level of participation: non-participating Commissioners Emeriti have no filing obligation, but those who participate in meetings may trigger filing requirements if their activities meet the criteria for 'participating in the making of a governmental decision' under California regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes both the original incoming request (dated Sept 4, 1984) and the FPPC's response (dated Oct 12, 1984). OCR quality is good. The response letter provides clear conditional guidance based on level of participation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is there any applicable filing requirement for the newly created position of Commissioner Emeritus to the Parks and Recreation Commission?

CONCLUSION: If the Commissioners Emeriti do not participate in Commission meetings, they would have no filing responsibility. However, if they participate in the meetings, the extent of their participation could trigger a filing obligation under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(c)(2), which requires filing if they advise, make recommendations, conduct research, prepare reports, or present analyses that are intended to influence governmental decisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to La Mesa's inquiry regarding filing responsibilities for newly created Commissioner Emeritus positions, concluding that filing obligations depend on whether the individuals actually participate in Commission meetings and the extent of that participation.
